codeReview.summary: Implementation is functional and meets requirements. Created centralized game score persistence in src/lib/gameScores.ts with proper localStorage integration and MasteryDashboard display. All three games (FlagFrenzy, InstructionHangman, RegisterRally) properly call updateGameScore on completion.

issues.description: Duplicate GameScore interface and loadGameScores function - GamesHub.tsx defines its own local versions instead of importing from @/lib/gameScores. While not a functional bug (both use the same localStorage key 'acs2906_gameScores'), this violates DRY principle and could cause maintenance issues. The local interface also lacks totalCorrect/totalWrong fields present in the centralized GameScore interface.

Silent error handling in loadGameScores() catches all errors and returns empty object. While this prevents app crashes, it could make debugging difficult if localStorage operations fail. Consider logging errors in development mode.
Silent error handling in saveGameScores() - localStorage.setItem errors are caught and ignored. If storage is full or disabled, the user loses data with no feedback.

sharedStateObservations.observation: GamesHub.tsx was created by a previous feature (games-hub) and has its own localStorage utilities that duplicate the new centralized gameScores.ts. This predates the game-persistence feature and wasn't addressed when game-persistence was implemented. No convention in AGENTS.md or architecture.md specifically addresses localStorage utility placement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: GamesHub.tsx:114-126 defines local GameScore interface and loadGameScores function; src/lib/gameScores.ts exports the same functionality

summary: Game persistence implementation is complete and functional. Scores persist in localStorage under 'acs2906_gameScores' key and display correctly on MasteryDashboard. All three games properly update scores on completion. The main issue is pre-existing code duplication in GamesHub.tsx which should be refactored to use the centralized gameScores.ts utilities, but this is non-blocking since both implementations use the same localStorage key and data remains consistent.
